agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with a sub-2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Spans ordering, dispatch, fulfillment, tracking, and post-delivery refund processing.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Fulfillment — covers eligibility determination, express order lifecycle, SLA tracking, driver assignment, real-time tracking, and express-specific refund processing. Touches but does not own: Driver Management (rating), Warehouse/Inventory, Billing/Payments, Customer Communication.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option promising fulfillment within a 2-hour window from order placement, available to eligible customers for a surcharge." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects express delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery speed. Not subject to express refund." },
      { "term": "Express Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period within which an express delivery must be completed. UNRESOLVED: whether this starts at order placement or at driver pickup." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The requirement that an express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of at least 4.0 stars who is qualified to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility Zone", "definition": "The area within 10km of a warehouse from which a customer may select express delivery. UNRESOLVED: whether this is geodesic (straight-line) or road-network distance." },
      { "term": "Replacement Assignment Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancels mid-delivery during which a replacement driver must be assigned before an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address", "definition": "A delivery address provided by the customer that is incorrect or undeliverable. Disqualifies the order from express refund eligibility. UNRESOLVED: who determines this and by what criteria." },
      { "term": "Express Refund", "definition": "A goodwill credit of the express fee ($12.99) issued when the express window is missed, subject to exclusions. Not a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "SLA Clock", "definition": "The countdown timer tracking remaining time in the express window. Start time is unresolved." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select Express Delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — fulfillment within the express window is impossible beyond this radius. Allowing ineligible orders corrupts the SLA promise.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must reject express delivery selection when the delivery address is beyond 10km of every active warehouse."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint — the 2-hour window is unachievable if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a hard prerequisite for SLA feasibility.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver picks up an express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate the order (exact escalation path TBD)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or higher may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Business policy — the 4-star threshold is a quality gate that operations could adjust based on driver supply and performance data.",
      "testable_assertion": "The dispatch system must never assign an express order to a driver whose current rating is below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing policy — Finance or Product could change this amount. Not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the order total must include exactly the configured express fee on top of the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the express window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Financial integrity — the base fee covers actual delivery cost. Refunding it would mean the company absorbs fulfillment cost on top of the penalty, which was never agreed to by any stakeholder.",
      "testable_assertion": "An express refund transaction must credit exactly the express fee amount and must not affect the base delivery fee charge."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Causation integrity — the SLA miss is attributable to the customer, not the service. Refunding would create a moral hazard.",
      "testable_assertion": "If a delivery is flagged as 'wrong address', the system must block automatic express refund processing for that order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Express refunds must be processed within 3 business days of the SLA miss event.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational SLA — Finance could tighten or relax this window. Not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "An express refund initiated by an SLA miss must reach 'processed' status within 3 business days."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-express-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If no replacement is assigned within that window, an automatic express refund is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Customer experience policy — the 10-minute window and auto-refund are business decisions that could be adjusted.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express delivery driver cancels, the system must start a 10-minute countdown. If no replacement is dispatched within that window, the system must automatically initiate an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express customers must have access to real-time driver location tracking from pickup through delivery.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint. Could be degraded gracefully.",
      "testable_assertion": "From the moment a driver picks up an express order until delivery completion, the customer-facing app must display the driver's location with updates at an interval no greater than [TBD] seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "Boundary condition — must be clearly defined as inclusive (<=10km) or exclusive (<10km). Recommend inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible driver (4+ stars) is available at order time", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Should express option be hidden? Greyed out? Should customer be told why? Must not allow express selection if fulfillment is impossible." },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14 and delivers at hour 2, minute 1", "expected_behavior": "Pickup SLA was met (under 15 min), but express window was missed. Refund should apply since the overall 2-hour SLA was breached." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to a location beyond 10km", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Does this void the express SLA? Should the system block the address change or downgrade to standard?" },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:50 PM — does the 2-hour window span midnight?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Are there operating hours for express delivery? If the window spans into non-operating hours, what happens?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 110 of the 2-hour window — only 10 minutes remain", "expected_behavior": "Replacement assignment within 10 minutes is possible per BR-8, but the replacement cannot deliver in time. Refund should trigger immediately rather than waiting 10 minutes." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a subtly wrong address (e.g., wrong apartment number, building exists)", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: 'Wrong address' is not precisely defined. Need clear criteria for what constitutes wrong address vs. delivery difficulty." },
    { "scenario": "Two warehouses are within 10km — which one fulfills the order?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Warehouse selection logic for express is not defined. Nearest? Most stocked? Most drivers available?" },
    { "scenario": "Network outage prevents real-time tracking mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Does a tracking outage count as a service failure eligible for refund? Or is tracking best-effort like the SLA itself?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels within the 10-minute window", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Does the 10-minute clock reset? Or does it continue from the first cancellation? Recommend: clock does NOT reset — refund triggers at original 10-minute expiry." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed to a correct address but customer is unreachable at delivery time", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED: Does failed delivery due to customer unavailability count as a missed window? Recommend: no refund, analogous to wrong-address exclusion." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99/order) creates a new revenue stream but carries refund liability. Refund costs are bounded (express fee only, not base fee). Wrong-address exclusion and best-effort legal positioning limit financial exposure. Key risk: if SLA miss rate exceeds ~20%, the refund costs plus operational overhead could erode margins. The 3-business-day refund processing window provides cash flow buffer.",
    "operational": "Significant operational complexity: 15-minute pickup SLA requires pre-positioned or rapidly dispatchable drivers. 4-star driver requirement constrains the eligible driver pool — could create supply bottlenecks in low-density areas. 10-minute replacement window after cancellation demands robust real-time dispatch. Real-time tracking requires reliable GPS infrastructure. Express orders will likely need priority queueing that may impact standard delivery times.",
    "user": "Strong value proposition for time-sensitive customers. Real-time tracking and prominent SLA display build trust. However, the legal/PM contradiction on 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' creates a UX language problem — if marketing says 'guaranteed' but terms say 'best effort', customer trust erodes on first missed delivery. Driver cancellation handling (10-min replacement or auto-refund) is a strong customer protection.",
    "risk": "Primary risk is the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction — if unresolved, it creates legal liability, customer confusion, and inconsistent system behavior. Secondary risk: the 4-star driver pool may be too small during peak hours, causing systematic SLA misses. Third: 'wrong address' is undefined and will generate customer disputes.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Gain a premium fast-delivery option with refund protection, but face confusion if 'guarantee' language is inconsistent. Wrong-address disputes may create friction." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star minimum creates a tiered system — high-rated drivers get premium (presumably higher-paying) assignments. May incentivize rating gaming. 15-minute pickup pressure increases stress." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support sub-15-minute order preparation to enable pickup SLA. May need dedicated express staging areas." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle wrong-address disputes, SLA miss complaints, and tracking issues. Need clear playbooks for each scenario, especially the guarantee vs. best-effort distinction." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line but must track refund rate, process refunds within 3 days, and reconcile express fee vs. base fee separately." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must ensure all customer-facing language reflects best-effort positioning, not a hard guarantee. Advertising and terms of service must be aligned." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is critically low due to a direct contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / not a contractual guarantee') on the core product promise. This is not a minor terminology issue — it fundamentally changes the domain model: a guarantee implies a contractual SLA with enforceable refund rights, while best-effort implies discretionary goodwill. The system's refund logic, customer-facing language, and dispute resolution all depend on which interpretation wins. Additionally, the SLA clock start time is undefined, 'wrong address' has no formal definition, and several operational gaps (operating hours, driver unavailability, warehouse selection) remain open.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount and separation from base fee (all stakeholders agree)",
      "10km eligibility zone requirement (PM + Operations aligned)",
      "15-minute pickup SLA (Operations clearly owns this)",
      "4-star driver rating minimum (Operations clearly owns this)",
      "Base fee is never refunded (PM + Finance aligned)",
      "Real-time tracking requirement (Customer Success, no contradictions)"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this is a blocking contradiction",
      "When the 2-hour SLA clock starts (order placement vs. driver pickup)",
      "Definition and adjudication process for 'wrong address'",
      "Behavior when no express-eligible driver is available",
      "Operating hours for express delivery",
      "Whether auto-refund from BR-8 conflicts with the 3-business-day processing from BR-7",
      "10km measurement method (geodesic vs. road distance)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 4-star rating threshold is >= 4.0 (inclusive), not strictly > 4.0",
      "The 10km zone is measured from the customer's delivery address to the nearest warehouse",
      "Express refund means refund of the express fee only, per PM and Finance alignment",
      "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 2-hour express window are measured from order placement time",
      "'Business days' for refund processing excludes weekends and public holidays"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' and the refund is an entitlement for missed windows. Legal states this is 'best effort' only and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are mutually exclusive positions. The system cannot be both — it either enforces automatic refunds as a contractual right or processes them as discretionary goodwill. This affects: customer-facing language, refund automation logic, dispute resolution flows, and terms of service.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Product Management", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Adopt Legal's position — 'best effort' SLA, refund is discretionary goodwill, system recommends but human approves each refund",
        "B: Adopt PM's position — hard guarantee with automatic refund, accept the legal risk, update terms of service accordingly",
        "C: Hybrid — automatic refund as standard practice (satisfies PM/CX) but terms of service frame it as goodwill policy that can be revoked (satisfies Legal)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Most logistics companies operate this way — they auto-refund to keep customers happy but preserve legal discretion in ToS. However, this MUST be resolved by PM + Legal together before any implementation begins."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 2-hour express window start time is never defined. PM says 'within 2 hours' (of what?). Operations says pickup must happen within 15 minutes 'of placement.' If the 2-hour clock starts at placement, the driver has 1h45m for transit. If it starts at pickup, the driver has the full 2 hours. This 15-minute difference materially changes SLA miss rates and refund exposure.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Drivers", "Finance", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Clock starts at order placement (customer's perspective — they placed the order and expect delivery within 2 hours)",
        "B: Clock starts at driver pickup (operational perspective — transit time is what the driver controls)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — from the customer's perspective, the clock starts when they pay. This is industry standard for time-guaranteed delivery products. The 15-minute pickup SLA is then a sub-constraint within the 2-hour window, not a separate clock."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "'Wrong address' is cited as a refund exclusion by Finance but has no definition, no determination process, and no dispute resolution path. In practice, this will be the #1 source of customer support disputes. Who decides an address is wrong — the driver? The system? A support agent? What evidence is required?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Customers", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address in-app with photo evidence, support reviews within 24 hours",
        "B: System auto-detects via geocoding mismatch (address doesn't resolve to a valid location)",
        "C: Both — system catches obvious geocoding failures, driver flags field-level issues with evidence"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — automated geocoding catches most issues at order time (preventing them), while driver flagging handles edge cases in the field. Both must feed into a reviewable audit trail."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Customer Success states auto-refund triggers if no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a cancellation. Finance states refunds are processed within 3 business days. These may conflict — does 'auto-refund' mean instantly initiated (but processed over 3 days), or does it mean instant credit to the customer's account?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-initiate refund immediately, but actual credit processing takes up to 3 business days",
        "B: Instant account credit for auto-refunds, 3-day processing only for manually reviewed refunds"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-refund means the system initiates without human approval, but the financial processing follows standard 3-business-day rails. Customer sees 'refund initiated' immediately."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours are defined for express delivery. Can a customer place an express order at 11 PM? At 3 AM? Does the 2-hour window respect operating boundaries? This is critical for SLA miss rate calculations and driver availability planning.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Customers", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Define express operating hours (e.g., 8 AM – 10 PM)",
        "B: Express is available 24/7 wherever driver supply exists",
        "C: Express availability is dynamic — shown only when driver supply can support the SLA"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C provides the best customer experience (no false promises) but requires real-time supply modeling. Option A is simpler to implement initially. Need Operations input."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No specification for what happens when no express-eligible driver (4+ stars) is available at order time. Should the express option be hidden? Should the order queue and wait (risking the 15-minute pickup SLA)? Should the rating threshold be relaxed?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Customers", "Product Management"],
      "options": [
        "A: Hide express option when no eligible drivers are available within the zone",
        "B: Accept the order and attempt to find a driver (risk SLA miss)",
        "C: Show express as 'temporarily unavailable' with estimated availability time"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — never promise what you cannot deliver. The system should check eligible driver availability before showing the express option. Option C is a better UX but requires availability prediction."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
